name: Alto Alentejo Portuguese
notes: Alentejo dialect (Portalegre, Évora, Beja). Categorical features per DIALECT_PATTERNS / whitepaper5 (TigreGotico internal dialect research): (1) INTERVOCALIC /d/ DELETION — nada→[ˈnaɐ], vida→[ˈviɐ]; positional_graphemes d/intervocalic→[''] (empty string = deletion); (2) /ej/ → [e] MONOPHTHONG — leite→[ˈletɨ], primeiro→[pɾiˈmeɾu]; (3) /ow/ → [o] MONOPHTHONG (shared with Lisbon); (4) meu-class simplification — /ew/ → [e] (meu→[me]); (5) nasal -em endings /ɐ̃j̃/ → [ẽ] class (bem→[bẽ], também→[tɐ̃ˈbẽ]) — encoded via allophone. NOTES-ONLY: word-final s+vowel sandhi voicing (→z) is partially handled by inherited pt-PT sandhi rules. NOTES-ONLY: meu digraph covers the ew→e class; full paradigm requires sandhi treatment beyond schema scope. Vowel breaking [ɛ͡ɐ] in certain lexical environments retained from prior spec.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Alentejo diverges mainly in open vowel quality and transitional seseo-adjacent features near the Spanish border

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: TigreGotico
sources.title: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md and whitepaper5: Phoneme-Level Dialect Transforms for European Portuguese
sources.notes: Internal dialect feature matrix and IPA transform inventory; pending external peer review

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
